jokes.text: And the groom wore a butt so perfect it could make an angel hang itself.
jokes.setup: Reading a wedding announcement that starts formally
jokes.punchline: Escalates to an absurdly over-the-top description of Ben's attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie-style hyperbolic language with unexpected dark twist. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: You wrote this, didn't you? - I did. - They cut it way down. - Thank God.
jokes.setup: Ben realizes Leslie wrote the over-the-top wedding announcement
jokes.punchline: Ben's relieved reaction that they edited out even more ridiculous content
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ben's exasperation with Leslie's enthusiasm.

jokes.text: It's like... Wha-a-a-at?
jokes.setup: Leslie describing Ben's surprised face
jokes.punchline: Leslie making an exaggerated dopey facial expression
jokes.explanation: Visual gag showing Leslie's affection through mockery. Moderately funny physical comedy.

jokes.text: She will make that stupid face!
jokes.setup: Ben planning to surprise Leslie with a gift
jokes.punchline: Ben's competitive excitement about making Leslie do the same face
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Ben's competitive side in their relationship.

jokes.text: the Richard and Lois Wallenberg Foundation Foundation... dedicated to the idea that all human beings deserve a chance to hear about foundations.
jokes.setup: Standard NPR-style foundation sponsor credits
jokes.punchline: Circular absurdity of a foundation for promoting awareness of foundations
jokes.explanation: Clever parody of NPR sponsor language with recursive absurdity.

jokes.text: I believe that is the Eagleton-Pawnee merger, n'est-ce pas?
jokes.setup: Co-hosts discussing the town merger
jokes.punchline: August's pretentious correction with unnecessary French phrase
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment of August's pretentious Eagleton personality.

jokes.text: I'd like to apologize to our listeners for my co-host's sudden and violent outburst just now.
jokes.setup: August made a mild correction
jokes.punchline: Derry dramatically overreacting to call it a 'violent outburst'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke showing the passive-aggressive tension between hosts.

jokes.text: Absorb the aggression, Derry. Absorb the aggression.
jokes.setup: Tension building between the radio co-hosts
jokes.punchline: August's condescending meditation-speak to deal with conflict
jokes.explanation: Shows August's new-age pretentiousness while building the co-host dynamic.

jokes.text: such a big, quiet, arcane ball of fun
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to compliment the merged radio show
jokes.punchline: Her description accidentally highlights how boring and obscure the show is
jokes.explanation: Leslie's attempt at politeness backfires in a character-appropriate way.

jokes.text: I haven't seen Pawneeans this mad since Frankie's discontinued their pizza-stuffed-crust pizza.
jokes.setup: Leslie describing the anger over the merger
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to anger over an absurdly unhealthy discontinued food item
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pawnee absurdity - pizza-stuffed-crust pizza is hilariously excessive and on-brand.

jokes.text: It was a pizza whose crust was stuffed with little pizzas.
jokes.setup: Already absurd pizza-stuffed-crust pizza
jokes.punchline: Even more ridiculous detail that the crust contained tiny full pizzas
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that takes an already absurd concept to an even more ridiculous level.

jokes.text: Scoff.
jokes.setup: August disagreeing with Leslie's assessment
jokes.punchline: August literally saying the word 'scoff' instead of scoffing
jokes.explanation: Perfectly pretentious character moment - August is so affected he verbalizes actions rather than doing them.

jokes.text: Did you just say the word 'scoff'?
jokes.setup: August saying 'scoff' literally
jokes.punchline: Derry's incredulous reaction calling out the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Necessary reaction beat that highlights the absurdity but isn't particularly clever on its own.

jokes.text: This twerp has a name. And it's August N'gutu Liebowitz-Clementine.
jokes.setup: August responding to being called a 'fatuous twerp'
jokes.punchline: His overly elaborate, pretentious full name
jokes.explanation: The hyphenated, multicultural name perfectly captures Eagleton pretentiousness.

jokes.text: Like, was it a hypnosis accident or something, where they put Gayle under and made her fall in love with Larry and never said the magic word to snap her out of it?
jokes.setup: Ben wondering how Larry got such a beautiful wife
jokes.punchline: Elaborate hypnosis conspiracy theory to explain the mismatch
jokes.explanation: Creative and elaborate explanation for the Larry/Gayle mystery that's been a running theme.

jokes.text: So, like a banana made of spiders?
jokes.setup: Looking at a child's drawing of a dog
jokes.punchline: Ron's hilariously accurate description of the terrible artwork
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson line - brutally honest, unexpectedly poetic, and hilariously specific visual.

jokes.text: I've said it before and I'll say it again, children are terrible artists. And artists are crooks.
jokes.setup: Ron commenting on bad child artwork
jokes.punchline: Escalates from insulting children to condemning all artists
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron philosophy that starts reasonable and escalates to his broader worldview. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Why lie? I'm shopping. Back in an hour.
jokes.setup: April looking for Donna at work
jokes.punchline: Donna's brutally honest note about abandoning work to go shopping
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna moment - completely honest about not caring about work responsibilities.

jokes.text: Kapow!
jokes.setup: Racist caller making inflammatory comment about Eagletonians
jokes.punchline: Caller adding sound effect to emphasize their terrible point
jokes.explanation: Weak joke relying on the absurdity of adding sound effects to bigotry rather than clever writing.

jokes.text: Well, that's what the merger is. Oh. Yeah, then I hate the merger.
jokes.setup: Caller saying they like the merger but hate that Eagleton was forced to join
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal when they realize that IS the merger
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective logic joke about not understanding what you're supporting.

jokes.text: I don't know who this is, but I agree. - It's Andy! - Hey. I'm on the radio!
jokes.setup: Leslie praising an anonymous caller's support
jokes.punchline: Revelation that it's Andy, plus his excitement about being on radio
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andy's enthusiasm and Leslie's need for support.

jokes.text: Thanks for nothing, Donna Meagle. Booooo! With five 'O's.
jokes.setup: April reading her fake Yelp review
jokes.punchline: Specifying the exact number of O's in her 'boo' for maximum pettiness
jokes.explanation: Good detail showing April's commitment to passive-aggressive pettiness.

jokes.text: Podcasts are great. Radio is boring.
jokes.setup: Tom trying to comfort Leslie about bad radio reception
jokes.punchline: Insulting the medium they're currently featured on
jokes.explanation: Mild meta-humor and character-appropriate technology preference from Tom.

jokes.text: Andy, this is a picture of us. This is a picture of an elderly couple.
jokes.setup: Andy commenting that Leslie looks old in a picture
jokes.punchline: Leslie pointing out it's not even a picture of them
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his classic obliviousness and Leslie's exasperation.

jokes.text: Whoo! I'm getting the epiphany sweats.
jokes.setup: Leslie having a realization about using the elderly couple
jokes.punchline: Leslie's physical manifestation of excitement as 'epiphany sweats'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie-ism - turning excitement into a specific physical symptom. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Just thinking about the new Star Wars sequel. I'm afraid they're gonna rely too heavily on CGI. And I'm carrying it all in my shoulders.
jokes.setup: Ben explaining why he's tense and needs a massage
jokes.punchline: His nerdy worry about movie production affecting him physically
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben character moment - nerdy concerns having real physical effects on him.

jokes.text: Larry, would you like to get a couples massage with me?
jokes.setup: Ben unable to find Leslie for their planned couples massage
jokes.punchline: Having to ask Larry to be his massage partner instead
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment as Ben's romantic plan gets awkwardly redirected.

jokes.text: Last time we scared an old person, Larry had a heart attack. That was awesome. He farted so much.
jokes.setup: Andy worried about surprising elderly people
jokes.punchline: Fondly remembering Larry's medical emergency because of the flatulence
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy - finding joy in inappropriate aspects of traumatic events.

jokes.text: I'm now just realizing how misleading this giant check is.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to explain they're not giving the elderly couple money
jokes.punchline: Realizing she's holding a large novelty check while saying this
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag that would work well on screen with Leslie's typical lack of foresight.

jokes.text: I'm about to come down on this dude like Thor's hammer Mjolnir. I'm about to go Mjolnir on his ass!
jokes.setup: Donna angry about a bad Yelp review
jokes.punchline: Using Norse mythology references to threaten internet revenge
jokes.explanation: Good Donna moment mixing pop culture with her fierce personality, though not exceptional.

jokes.text: Dear frozen yogurt, you are the celery of desserts. Be ice cream or be nothing. Zero stars.
jokes.setup: Ron reading one of his complaint letters
jokes.punchline: Poetic condemnation of frozen yogurt with vegetable metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ron line - perfectly captures his disdain for health food with an unexpectedly clever metaphor. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: I watched Enchanted three times to get all the details right. And then another two times, because that movie is amazing.
jokes.setup: Ben explaining his preparation for Leslie's surprise
jokes.punchline: Admitting he watched it extra times because he genuinely loved it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ben's romantic side and unexpected enjoyment of Disney movies.

jokes.text: Good sir, tally ho. God, that... from the movie! That is definitely from the movie.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to recreate romantic movie moments
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt at period dialogue and immediate self-congratulation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with Ben's awkward roleplay enthusiasm

jokes.text: They're like the old version of iTunes. We're like the new version of iTunes, baby. We're gonna be here forever. Oh, my God. I'm gonna die someday.
jokes.setup: Tom making an analogy about old vs. young people using technology
jokes.punchline: His confidence immediately crumbling into existential dread
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from tech-bro confidence to sudden mortality panic. Very Tom-like emotional whiplash.

jokes.text: Hey, no touching. This is not your birthday.
jokes.setup: Elderly couple posing for recreation of wedding photo
jokes.punchline: Wife refusing physical affection except on specific occasions
jokes.explanation: Good establishment of the couple's marital dysfunction with specific, oddly formal rules.

jokes.text: Don't give him any water. He does this annoying thing where every time he takes a drink of water he goes, 'Ah...' She does this annoying thing where every time she takes a drink of water she is an old nag.
jokes.setup: Wife complaining about husband's water-drinking sounds
jokes.punchline: Husband's much harsher counter-complaint
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how their complaints differ in severity and meanness.

jokes.text: Just took some ludes to kind of smooth it out, so I'm right in that sweet spot. Got about 20 minutes before I either get really tired or really horny, so let's roll.
jokes.setup: Joan explaining she took uppers and needs to go on TV
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning taking quaaludes and their sexual side effects
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about elderly drug use that creates uncomfortable urgency for the TV appearance.

jokes.text: You look like a leather glove got caught in a lawnmower.
jokes.setup: Rosie looking at Doug before TV appearance
jokes.punchline: Her vivid and specific insult about his appearance
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and visual insult with great economy. Very quotable.

jokes.text: You look like a leather glove got caught in a lawnmower.
jokes.setup: Rosie commenting on Doug's appearance before TV
jokes.punchline: Vivid and specific insult about his weathered appearance
jokes.explanation: Creative and specific visual insult that's both cruel and poetic. Very memorable imagery.

jokes.text: You tried to adopt a cat last week and I turned you down, because I thought it was too sad for you to have six cats. Then you tried to blow up my spot online. You're fired.
jokes.setup: Donna confronting Kyle about the bad Yelp review
jokes.punchline: Revealing the review was revenge for her rejecting his cat adoption
jokes.explanation: Good resolution that connects the Yelp mystery to earlier pet adoption subplot.

jokes.text: You're not my boss, I work for the city attorney's office. You're fired.
jokes.setup: Kyle pointing out Donna can't fire him
jokes.punchline: Donna firing him anyway despite having no authority
jokes.explanation: Shows Donna's forceful personality but the joke itself is fairly predictable.

jokes.text: I had no prospects, and Dougie here had an alcohol problem. Plus the Cold War was happening, and frankly, we all thought we were gonna die.
jokes.setup: Asked about why they got married
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest list of unromantic reasons including geopolitical fear
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of expected romantic answer with historical context and brutal honesty.

jokes.text: Well, I figured I could really tick off my parents by shacking up with some Pawnee trash.
jokes.setup: Doug explaining his motivation for marriage
jokes.punchline: Admitting he married her specifically to rebel against his parents
jokes.explanation: Continues the anti-romantic theme while adding class warfare element typical of Pawnee/Eagleton divide.

jokes.text: I slept with Sharon in 1979. I used to get out of the shower in front of your brother on purpose.
jokes.setup: Interview going wrong as couple argues on TV
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire revelation of infidelities and inappropriate behavior
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of marital dysfunction with increasingly specific and uncomfortable revelations.

jokes.text: They eat, they sleep, they complain, and they watch Family Feud. Oh, my God, I wanna be an old person.
jokes.setup: Tom dismissing old people's lifestyle
jokes.punchline: Immediately realizing that sounds appealing to him
jokes.explanation: Good Tom moment showing his realization that the lazy lifestyle he mocks actually appeals to him.

jokes.text: Veganism is the sad result of a morally corrupt mind. Reconsider your life.
jokes.setup: Ron's complaint letter to vegan restaurant
jokes.punchline: His dramatic condemnation of dietary choices
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron - treating dietary choice as moral failing with dramatic language

jokes.text: Veganism is the sad result of a morally corrupt mind. Reconsider your life.
jokes.setup: Vegan restaurant owner confronting Ron about his letter
jokes.punchline: Ron's dramatically harsh condemnation of veganism as moral corruption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron philosophy - treating dietary choices as fundamental character flaws with dramatic language.

jokes.text: A vegan is gonna physically attack me? They're basically made of glass.
jokes.setup: Ron being warned about consequences of insulting vegans
jokes.punchline: Ron's dismissive assessment of vegans' physical capabilities
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron logic about nutrition affecting physical strength, delivered with perfect confidence.

jokes.text: It was a review of a funeral home that said 'Great first date spot.'
jokes.setup: Donna explaining how she identified Kyle as the reviewer
jokes.punchline: Kyle's inappropriately dark review of a funeral home
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny review that perfectly captures Kyle's social awkwardness and poor judgment.

jokes.text: Well, you overestimated how much I care what happens to Kyle.
jokes.setup: Donna saying she brought Kyle to see if April would confess
jokes.punchline: April's honest admission she doesn't care about Kyle at all
jokes.explanation: Classic April honesty about her lack of empathy. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Your lipstick looks weird. You're gonna want to shut your mouth right now.
jokes.setup: April and Donna agreeing to be honest with each other
jokes.punchline: April immediately testing this with an insult, Donna shutting it down
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing April testing boundaries and Donna setting limits.

jokes.text: Craig Middlebrooks. Samantha in the boardroom, Miranda in the bedroom. I know it's not ideal, but it's who I am.
jokes.setup: Young person introducing himself to the youth committee
jokes.punchline: Craig's oversharing about his personality being split between Sex and the City characters
jokes.explanation: Good character introduction showing Craig's dramatic oversharing and pop culture references.

jokes.text: Donna said she might be able to get Ginuwine. That's given me a reason to live.
jokes.setup: Craig describing the Unity Concert plans
jokes.punchline: Dramatically declaring that a musician booking gives him purpose
jokes.explanation: Good Craig moment showing his dramatic tendency to find existential meaning in pop culture.

jokes.text: Oh, my eff-ing God.
jokes.setup: Leslie seeing Ben's elaborate anniversary surprise
jokes.punchline: Leslie self-censoring her profanity in the moment of surprise
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment showing Leslie's tendency to self-censor even when overwhelmed.

jokes.text: I had a guy at the model store make a replica. He's a bigger Game of Thrones fan than you are.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining how she got the Iron Throne replica made
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning someone is an even bigger nerd than Ben
jokes.explanation: Mild joke about nerd hierarchy and Ben's obsession level being surpassed by someone else.

jokes.text: When you play the game of thrones, you win or you die. Yes. No. Yes. No!
jokes.setup: Ben and Leslie roleplaying with the Iron Throne
jokes.punchline: Their confused/excited back and forth while playing
jokes.explanation: Sweet couple moment with confused but enthusiastic roleplay

jokes.text: Come here. Come hither, peasant. Come here. Who are you? Our starship is in trouble.
jokes.setup: Ben and Leslie roleplaying with the Iron Throne
jokes.punchline: Leslie mixing Game of Thrones with Star Trek references
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's lack of nerd knowledge compared to Ben, mixing fantasy and sci-fi genres.
